description: Corrected Adult Bradycardia With a Pulse FSM per AHA 2025 ACLS guidelines. Fixes: observe_and_treat_causes and expert_consultation are no longer dead-end final states. Added AV block type classification pathway, atropine contraindications for infranodal blocks and transplanted hearts (Class III — Harm), transcutaneous pacing with capture confirmation, sedation/analgesia requirement for TCP, dopamine/epinephrine infusion details, transvenous pacing pathway, underlying cause (H's & T's) tracking, and full context + guards.

thresholds._comment: Clinical thresholds per AHA 2025. Modify only under medical director review.

atropine.note: Atropine acts at AV node via vagal blockade. Ineffective and potentially harmful for infranodal blocks and transplanted (denervated) hearts.

transcutaneous_pacing.note: Pacing is painful — sedation (e.g., midazolam) and analgesia (e.g., fentanyl) must be provided to conscious patients.

context._comment: Runtime state — populated by the debriefing engine as events are processed.

guards._comment: Guard definitions — evaluated at event firing time. Violations push to context.deviations[].

atropine_max_dose_not_exceeded.description: AHA 2025: Maximum total atropine dose is 3 mg IV. Do not exceed.

deviation_if_violated.message: Atropine dose exceeds maximum total of 3 mg. AHA 2025: Do not administer more than 3 mg total atropine in a bradycardia event.

atropine_min_interval.description: AHA 2025: Atropine repeat doses should not be given more frequently than every 3 minutes.

deviation_if_violated.message: Atropine repeat dose given less than 3 minutes after previous dose. AHA 2025: Repeat every 3-5 minutes.

atropine_not_for_infranodal_block.description: AHA 2025: Atropine is ineffective and potentially harmful for Mobitz Type II second-degree AV block and third-degree (complete) AV block with wide QRS (infranodal origin). Do NOT delay transcutaneous pacing to give atropine in these patients.

deviation_if_violated.message: Atropine administered for confirmed infranodal block (Mobitz Type II or complete AV block with wide QRS). AHA 2025: Atropine is ineffective and may worsen these blocks or precipitate asystole. Proceed directly to transcutaneous pacing.

atropine_not_for_transplant.description: AHA 2025 / ACC/AHA/HRS Class III — HARM: Atropine is contraindicated in transplanted (denervated) hearts. It is ineffective and has caused paradoxical bradycardia and high-degree AV block.

deviation_if_violated.message: Atropine given to a heart transplant patient. AHA/ACC/HRS Class III — HARM. Transplanted hearts are denervated and cannot respond to atropine. Risk of paradoxical worsening. Use transcutaneous pacing, dopamine, or epinephrine instead.

tcp_sedation_before_pacing.description: AHA 2025: Transcutaneous pacing is painful. Sedation and analgesia must be provided to conscious patients whenever feasible.

deviation_if_violated.message: Transcutaneous pacing initiated without documented sedation and analgesia. AHA 2025: TCP is painful — provide sedation (e.g., midazolam) and analgesia (e.g., fentanyl) to conscious patients.

tcp_mechanical_capture_verified.description: AHA 2025: Electrical capture on monitor does NOT guarantee mechanical capture (perfusion). Central pulse must be confirmed — palpate femoral pulse matching paced rate, or use ultrasound/pulse oximetry waveform.

deviation_if_violated.message: Transcutaneous pacing electrical capture noted but mechanical capture not confirmed. AHA 2025: Always verify mechanical capture via central pulse palpation (femoral) or point-of-care ultrasound — electrical capture alone does not ensure perfusion.

atropine_before_tcp_in_compromise.description: AHA 2025: For symptomatic bradycardia with compromise where atropine is expected to work (nodal blocks, sinus bradycardia), atropine should be the first-line drug before TCP unless atropine is contraindicated.
atropine_before_tcp_in_compromise.condition: context.atropine_dose_count >= 1 OR context.infranodal_block_confirmed == true OR context.heart_transplant_patient == true

deviation_if_violated.message: Transcutaneous pacing initiated without a prior atropine attempt. AHA 2025: Atropine is first-line for symptomatic bradycardia unless contraindicated (infranodal block, transplanted heart).

actions._comment: What the engine updates in context when each event fires.
actions.record_av_block_type: Set context.av_block_type = event.av_block_type. If Mobitz Type II or Third-degree with wide QRS → set context.infranodal_block_confirmed = true.
actions.record_compromise_signs: Update context.compromise_signs from event. Set context.hemodynamically_compromised = true if any sign is present.
actions.record_atropine: context.atropine_dose_count += 1; context.atropine_total_dose_mg += 1; context.atropine_last_given_ms = event.timestamp_ms; context.atropine_result = event.result.
actions.record_tcp_initiation: context.tcp_initiated = true; context.tcp_rate_bpm = event.rate_bpm; context.tcp_output_mA = event.output_mA.
actions.push_deviation: Push { rule, severity, timestamp_ms, detail } into context.deviations[].

initial_assessment.description: Assess appropriateness for clinical condition. Heart rate typically < 50 bpm if bradyarrhythmia. Maintain patent airway and provide oxygen if hypoxemic. Assist breathing with positive-pressure ventilation as necessary. Attach cardiorespiratory monitor. Monitor pulse oximetry and blood pressure. Establish IV access. Obtain 12-lead ECG — do not delay therapy for ECG if patient is compromised.

AV_BLOCK_TYPE_IDENTIFIED._comment: Classifying the block type early is critical — determines whether atropine is appropriate.

HEART_TRANSPLANT_IDENTIFIED._comment: Flag transplant patients early — atropine is Class III HARM in denervated hearts.

check_cardiopulmonary_compromise.description: Evaluate if bradycardia persists with cardiopulmonary compromise: Hypotension? Acutely altered mental status? Signs of shock? Ischemic chest discomfort? Acute heart failure? If ANY sign present → immediate intervention. If NONE → observe and treat reversible causes.

classify_block_for_treatment.description: CRITICAL DECISION POINT: Classify the block type to determine whether atropine is appropriate. Nodal blocks (sinus bradycardia, Mobitz Type I / Wenckebach, first-degree AV block) → try atropine first. Infranodal blocks (Mobitz Type II, complete AV block with wide QRS) → skip atropine, go directly to TCP. Transplanted heart → skip atropine, go directly to TCP or infusion.

BLOCK_IS_NODAL._comment: Nodal: sinus bradycardia, first-degree AV, Mobitz Type I. Atropine expected to work.

BLOCK_IS_INFRANODAL._comment: Infranodal: Mobitz Type II, Third-degree/complete AV block with wide QRS. Atropine likely ineffective and potentially harmful — go directly to TCP.

BLOCK_TYPE_UNKNOWN._comment: If block type unclear, attempt atropine once while preparing TCP. Do not delay pacing.

TRANSPLANT_PATIENT._comment: Denervated heart — skip atropine entirely. Go directly to TCP / chronotropic infusion.

administer_atropine.description: First-line pharmacologic treatment for symptomatic nodal bradycardia with compromise. Dose: 1 mg IV bolus. Repeat every 3-5 minutes. Maximum total dose: 3 mg. While giving atropine, prepare transcutaneous pacing — do not delay TCP if atropine fails.

ATROPINE_EFFECTIVE._comment: HR increased to acceptable level with resolution of compromise signs.

ATROPINE_INEFFECTIVE_MAX_DOSE_REACHED._comment: 3 mg total given, no response → escalate.

ATROPINE_PARTIALLY_EFFECTIVE._comment: Some improvement but compromise persists — may repeat if under max dose while preparing TCP.

ATROPINE_GIVEN_FOR_INFRANODAL_BLOCK._comment: Flags deviation and proceeds — engine does not block action, only records the deviation.

second_line_interventions.description: Atropine ineffective, contraindicated, or infranodal/transplant patient. Initiate ALL of the following as appropriate and simultaneously: (1) Transcutaneous pacing (TCP) — preferred immediate intervention. (2) Dopamine infusion 5-20 mcg/kg/min OR Epinephrine infusion 2-10 mcg/min as bridge to pacing or while pacing is set up. Prepare for transvenous pacing. Seek expert consultation.

tcp_setup.description: Set up transcutaneous pacing. Provide sedation and analgesia to conscious patients before or during TCP — it is painful. Set pacing rate to 60-80 bpm in demand mode. Start output at minimum mA and increase until electrical capture achieved.

DOPAMINE_INFUSION_STARTED._comment: Dopamine or epinephrine can be started concurrently as a bridge while TCP is being set up.

tcp_titrating.description: TCP in progress. Increase output (mA) gradually until electrical capture confirmed: pacer spike followed by wide QRS on monitor. Then confirm MECHANICAL capture: palpate central (femoral) pulse at paced rate, or use POCUS/pulse oximetry. Add safety margin of 5-10 mA above capture threshold.

ELECTRICAL_CAPTURE_WITHOUT_MECHANICAL_CH._comment: Flagging when team documents electrical capture only without verifying mechanical capture.

tcp_maintaining.description: Successful transcutaneous pacing with confirmed mechanical capture. Patient is being bridged. Continue monitoring. Treat reversible causes. Prepare for transvenous pacing as definitive therapy. Seek expert consultation.

tcp_failed.description: Transcutaneous pacing failed to achieve capture at maximum output. Escalate: Expert consultation urgently. Transvenous pacing is the definitive next step. Continue chronotropic infusions as bridge.

transvenous_pacing.description: Definitive pacing therapy. Indicated when transcutaneous pacing fails or for persistent symptomatic bradycardia requiring prolonged pacing. Requires expert consultation and procedure setup. Continue TCP and chronotropic infusions as bridge until transvenous pacing is operational.

observe_and_treat_causes.description: Patient is stable (no compromise) OR stabilized after intervention. Identify and treat all reversible underlying causes: Hs and Ts (hypoxia, hypovolemia, hydrogen ion/acidosis, hypo/hyperkalemia, hypothermia, hypoglycemia, toxins/drug overdose, tamponade, tension pneumothorax, thrombosis, hypothyroidism, myocardial ischemia). Support ABCs. Oxygen if hypoxemic. 12-lead ECG if not yet obtained. Continuous monitoring. Consider expert consultation for recurrent or complex bradycardia.

BRADYCARDIA_RECURS_WITH_COMPROMISE._comment: Patient re-develops compromise — restart the decision tree.

cardiac_arrest_pathway.description: Patient has deteriorated to pulseless cardiac arrest. Hand off to AHA 2025 Adult Cardiac Arrest Algorithm (aha_2025_cpr_engine). This FSM terminates and the cardiac arrest FSM takes over.
cardiac_arrest_pathway._comment: Trigger aha_2025_cpr_engine FSM with ARREST_RECOGNIZED event.

terminated.description: Patient stabilized and discharged from acute care, or debriefing session complete.

deviation_severity_levels.CRITICAL: Directly contradicts AHA 2025 guideline with potential for patient death. Must be addressed in debriefing.
deviation_severity_levels.HIGH: Significant protocol deviation. Clear guideline violation. Requires debriefing discussion.
deviation_severity_levels.MEDIUM: Notable deviation. May impact patient outcome. Recommend debriefing review.
deviation_severity_levels.LOW: Minor deviation or soft recommendation. Discuss as improvement opportunity.
deviation_severity_levels.ADVISORY: Informational. No clear protocol violation but worth noting.
